title: ODI Matrix — NAICS 221114 Solar Electric Power Generation

sections.title: Step 1 — Error statements gathered
sections.content:
- Market needs from Step 06 JTBD: **18** (derived from references in FIT file — needs #1, #2, #4, #5, #7, #8, #9, #10, #11, #13, #14 explicitly cited with opportunity cues)
- Product-related needs from Step 10: **9** (P1–P9, after P10 removed by litmus test)
- **Total entries: 27**

---

sections.title: Step 2 — Satisfaction recalibration (incumbent-anchored)
sections.content:
### Incumbents in 221114

| Tier | Incumbent | Role | Strengths | Weaknesses |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Solar-EPC peers | BayWa r.e. · Enerparc · Goldbeck Solar | PV+BESS hybrid EPC at 20–100 MW | Scale procurement, repeat FGW TR8 delivery, VPP partnerships | Multi-project queue; hybrid cert dossier coupling |
| BESS integrators | Sungrow PowerTitan · Tesla Megapack · CATL EnerC | L2 container supply | OEM firmware, BMS quality, priority allocation | Split contract if not EPC'd |
| PCS | SMA · Sungrow · Power Electronics · Ingeteam | 4-quadrant inverters | Q(U)/cos-phi, <100 ms response | MV-bay interop per site |
| Cert bodies | TÜV NORD · Dekra (FGW-accredited) | Type B plant cert | Recognized by DSOs | 6–12 mo lead |
| VPP/aggregators | Next Kraftwerke · Entrix · Flexpower | EMS dispatch + revenue stack | Mature market access | Single-site owners under-served |

### Recalibration moves vs. Step 06 originals

| Direction | Needs | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| ↓ Satisfaction | P1 Li thermal cascade, P9 CAPEX band, M4 DSO capacity, M9 TR8 slip | Research shows incumbents still leave gap — field cascade events + DSO denials persist at peer scale |
| ≈ Satisfaction | M5 transformer lead, M8 Feuerwehr, M13 BMS | Peer incumbents credibly at mid-level |
| ↑ Satisfaction | M16 FCR prequal, M17 revenue stack | VPP partners (Next Kraftwerke, Entrix) close gap for peers |

---

**Formula spot-check (Q1):** P1 = 10 + max(10−4,0) = 16 ✓ · M4 = 9.5 + max(9.5−3,0) = 16 ✓ · M14 = 9 + max(9−4,0) = 14 ✓ · P4 = 8 + max(8−4.5,0) = 11.5 ✓ · M7 = 7 + max(7−6,0) = 8 ✓

sections.title: Step 4 — Market ODI summary
sections.content:
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Market | PV+BESS Solar IPPs |
| NAICS | 221114 |
| Total needs | 27 |
| Underserved count (imp>7 ∧ sat<5) | **14** |
| Overserved count (imp<3 ∧ sat>7) | **0** |
| Avg opportunity score | **11.39** |
| Top-5 opportunities | P1, M4, M9, M14, P3 |

### Top-5 error statements

1. Minimize the likelihood of Li-ion thermal runaway propagation from a single failed cell across the container rack (16.00)
2. Minimize the likelihood of DSO capacity denial at the planned feeder, forcing a grid-upgrade cost that invalidates the project (16.00)
3. Minimize the likelihood of FGW TR8 plant-certificate slip landing on the critical path and delaying COD (15.00)
4. Minimize the likelihood of a cell-level thermal event going undetected until propagation reaches neighbouring modules (14.00)
5. Minimize the likelihood of VdS / Feuerwehr separation-wall requirement triggering once total storage exceeds the 3 MWh Li cluster threshold (14.00)

---

sections.title: Step 5 — Cross-check vs. Feature-Market Fit (Step 09)
sections.content:
| Check | Value | Flag |
|---|---|---|
| ODI avg | 11.39 | in 8–12 band |
| Feature-Fit avg (F1,F2,F3,F4,F5,F8) | 6.45 | in 4–7 band |
| `ODI>12 ∧ FFit<4` | — | no flag |
| `ODI<8 ∧ FFit>7` | — | no flag |
| **Result** | no inconsistency logged | PASS |

**Directional read:** Top ODI opportunities map cleanly to top-scoring features. FGW TR8 slip (M9, opp=15) ↔ F5 Grid-Code Certification Delivery (fit=7.78). Cell-thermal (M14, opp=14) ↔ F1 Containerized Li-ion BESS Integration (reliability=9, safety=8). BESS mis-sizing (M1, opp=13) ↔ F4 EMS (cost=9, stress=7).

---

sections.title: Neo4j write preview
sections.content:
```
(Market {naics_code:"221114", run_id}) SET
  odi_total_needs=27, odi_underserved_count=14, odi_overserved_count=0,
  odi_avg_opportunity_score=11.39, odi_top_opportunities=<JSON>
(Market) -[:has_opportunity_score]-> (OpportunityScore) × 27
  key = { error_statement[:80], naics_code, run_id }
```

---

Minimize the likelihood of grid-code misclassification between VDE-AR-N Type A and Type B
Minimize the likelihood of MV transformer procurement lead-time exceeding the critical-path schedule
Minimize the likelihood of container-footprint overrun exceeding the 40-80 m2/MWh site envelope once fire-clearance setbacks are applied
Minimize the likelihood of cycle-life erosion below 6000 cycles at 80% DoD from aggressive revenue-stack cycling
Minimize the likelihood of roundtrip-efficiency degradation below 85% AC-AC at first-year testing invalidating pro-forma revenue assumptions

Minimize the likelihood of voltage-ride-through or reactive-power retest after commissioning

incumbents_used.name: Enerparc
weaknesses: BESS-retrofit track record younger than core PV

adjustments.reason: Li-cascade field events still observed even in tier-1 peer projects
DSO saturation is systemic in rural DE MV feeders — incumbents cannot resolve
Peer cert-slip rate remains elevated due to FGW-body capacity
VPP partnerships (Next Kraftwerke, Entrix) meaningfully close the prequal gap for tier-1 peers
Revenue-stack optimization is table-stakes among VPP partners for tier-1 peers
